title: Understand: organization in the human body
description: Check your understanding of organization in the human body in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Understand: organization in the human body

assessment_items.question_content:
The circulatory system includes veins, arteries, blood, and the heart. All of these parts are made up of cells.

**Which of the following is true about the cells in the circulatory system?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cells in the blood and heart have the same structure, but different functions.
Cells in the blood and heart have different, specialized structures and functions.
Each cell in the blood and heart is a unique type of cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
The digestive system includes the esophagus, stomach, large intestine, and small intestine. All of these parts are made up of cells.

**Which of the following is true about the cells in the digestive system?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cells in the stomach and large intestine have the same structure, but different functions.
Cells in the stomach and large intestine have different, specialized structures and functions.
Each cell in the stomach and large intestine is a unique type of cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
The nervous system includes the brain, nerves, and spinal cord. All of these parts are made up of cells.

**Which of the following is true about the cells in the nervous system?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cells in the brain and spinal cord have the same structure, but different functions.
Cells in the brain and spinal cord have different, specialized structures and functions.
Each cell in the brain and spinal cord is a unique type of cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What are tissues made up of?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: cells
organs
organ systems
cells
organs
organ systems

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these is made up of organs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a cell
a tissue
an organ system

assessment_items.question_content:
The skin is an organ that protects the body and helps with sensing the environment.

**Which of the following is true about skin?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The skin is made up of a single type of tissue.
The skin is made up of different tissues that carry out the same function.
The skin is made up of tissues with different functions that work together.

assessment_items.question_content:
The brain is an organ in the nervous system.

**Which of the following is true about the brain?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The brain is made up of a single type of tissue.
The brain is made up of different tissues that carry out the same function.
The brain is made up of tissues with different functions that work together.

assessment_items.question_content:
The kidneys are organs in the excretory system.

**Which of the following is true about the kidneys?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The kidneys are made up of a single type of tissue.
The kidneys are made up of different tissues that carry out the same function.
The kidneys are made up of tissues with different functions that work together.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement is true about organ systems in the human body?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Organ systems carry out unique functions that help the body live and grow.
Each organ system works completely alone.
If one organ system fails, another will take its place.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement is true about organ systems in the human body?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Organ systems interact to allow the body to live and grow.
If one organ system fails, a different organ system can take its place.
All organ systems carry out the same functions in the human body.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement is true about organ systems in the human body?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Each organ system carries out a unique function in the human body.
If one organ system fails due to a disease, another will take its place.
Each organ system works completely alone as it helps the body live and grow.
